title: narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. pallidiflorus
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. pallidiflorus, also known as the pale daffodil, is a subspecies of the wild daffodil (Narcissus pseudonarcissus) plant. It belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family and is known for its delicate white or pale yellow flowers. The pale daffodil has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which have been reported to have health benefits including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing.
